points.text: Erasca Inc. (ERAS) stock fell 48% ($9.25) following RevMed's patent infringement accusation regarding Erasca's pan-RAS molecular glue ERAS-0015 and a patient death one month post-dosing.

Hagens Berman filed a securities class action against Erasca Inc. for the period Jan. 14, 2025 – Apr. 26, 2026, alleging misleading claims about ERAS-0015's equivalence to RevMed's RMC-6236 and intellectual property validity.
Verastem Oncology announced intent to evaluate VS-7375 (KRAS G12D inhibitor) in combination with ERAS-0015, an investigational pan-RAS molecular glue.

Erasca Inc. — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Pan-RAS molecular glue; RevMed accused Erasca of patent infringement and trade secret misappropriation; patient death reported one month after dosing; stock lost $2.8B market cap.

Verastem Oncology — VS-7375 + ERAS-0015 — KRAS G12D-mutated solid tumors — Announced intent to evaluate combination of oral KRAS G12D dual ON/OFF inhibitor with investigational pan-RAS molecular glue; Phase 1/2 TARGET-D 101 trial data showed 93% CA19-9 reduction in PDAC patients at 900 mg monotherapy.

Verastem Oncology — VS-7375 — KRAS G12D–mutated solid tumors — Phase 1/2 TARGET-D 101 trial: 93% (n=13/14) of heavily pretreated metastatic PDAC patients on 900 mg monotherapy achieved >50% CA19-9 reduction; combination with cetuximab showed deeper tumor reductions.
Verastem Oncology — ERAS-0015 — Pan-RAS indications — Investigational pan-RAS molecular glue identified for future combination evaluation with VS-7375 in KRAS G12D–mutated solid tumors.

Erasca Inc. — Pan-RAS molecular glue development — ERAS-0015 — Facing securities class action and IP dispute with Revolution Medicines; in-licensed patent family from Joyo includes one issued US patent and thirteen pending foreign applications.

Revolution Medicines — Intellectual property enforcement — RMC-6236 (competitor to ERAS-0015) — Accused Erasca of deceptively comparing ERAS-0015 40mg cohort to RMC-6236 400mg cohort and misappropriating trade secrets.

Verastem Oncology — KRAS G12D inhibitor and molecular glue combination strategy — VS-7375 + ERAS-0015 — Phase 1/2 TARGET-D 101 trial ongoing; preliminary data supports advancing combination with pan-RAS molecular glue ERAS-0015.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: Investigational (oral) — Population: RAS-mutant solid tumors including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma — Comparator: Alleged improper comparison to RMC-6236 400mg vs ERAS-0015 40mg — Outcome: Patient death reported one month post-dosing; IP validity challenged.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: Investigational — Population: KRAS G12D–mutated solid tumors (planned) — Comparator: None stated — Status: Identified for combination evaluation with VS-7375.

VS-7375 — Mechanism: Oral KRAS G12D dual ON/OFF inhibitor — Phase: 1/2 (TARGET-D 101) — Population: Metastatic PDAC, CRC, NSCLC with KRAS G12D mutation — Comparator: Monotherapy vs combination with cetuximab — Results: 93% CA19-9 reduction in PDAC at 900 mg; dose-dependent activity observed.

Erasca Inc. — Securities litigation — ERAS-0015 — Hagens Berman filed class action seeking investors who purchased stock Jan 14, 2025–Apr 26, 2026; market cap loss of $2.8B triggered by IP dispute and patient death disclosure.
Erasca — Securities class action litigation — ERAS-0015 — Stock lost $2.8 billion market cap; lawsuit alleges misleading statements on safety and IP; lead plaintiff deadline set for Aug 10, 2026.

Erasca Inc. — Intellectual Property dispute — ERAS-0015 — Revolution Medicines accused Erasca of patent infringement; Erasca claims in-licensed patent family from Joyo (1 issued US, 1 pending US, 1 issued foreign, 13 pending foreign patents).
